name: Western Union Money Order Only
description_short: Del Valle, TX location of Western Union Money Order Only at 2001 E Hwy 71 E, open daily 6AM–10PM for money orders and check cashing.
description_long:
Western Union Money Order Only is located at 2001 E Hwy 71 E in Del Valle, TX, operating as a standalone check-cashing and money order facility. The location maintains extended hours daily from 6AM to 10PM, making it accessible for residents of Del Valle who need fast money order services outside of traditional banking hours. This Del Valle branch serves the community with convenient, walk-in access to financial transfer services.

At this Del Valle, TX location, customers can send and receive money orders through Western Union's trusted network. The store specializes in check cashing and money transfer services. For specific questions about rates, accepted documents, or service options, call +1 512-385-8943 during business hours to speak with staff who can assist with your transaction.

If you're a Del Valle resident needing to send money quickly or cash a check, this Western Union Money Order Only location offers a straightforward alternative to bank branches. Bring a valid government-issued ID and your check or cash to complete a money order transaction. The 7-day operating schedule and early opening time (6AM) make it convenient for work commutes or errands in the Del Valle area.
address: 2001 E Hwy 71 E, Del Valle, TX 78617

company_info.headquarters: 2001 E Hwy 71 E, Del Valle, TX 78617

pros: Massive agent network of 500,000+ global locations with extensive U.S. presence at major retailers (Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Circle K, Kroger)
24/7 online initiation through WU.com with flexible in-store completion based on agent hours
Multi-language support (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) serving diverse customer populations
Physical cash pickup capability eliminating need for bank accounts—critical for unbanked populations
Fast transfer processing with real-time tracking capabilities for sent money
Loyalty rewards program (Western Union Rewards) available to frequent users
Specialized services including inmate payments, mobile top-ups, and bill payment options
cons: Money order services not available at all locations—website explicitly notes location-specific availability restrictions
No transparent fee structure displayed on location finder; historical pattern of high fees on international transfers
Operates solely as transfer intermediary without banking products, credit-building tools, or lending services
Orange Wallet payment method shown as temporarily unavailable, suggesting service reliability issues
In-store completion dependent on agent location hours, limiting true 24/7 access despite online-first capability
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers without traditional bank accounts needing physical cash access
Immigrant communities sending remittances internationally with multi-language support needs
Consumers in rural areas relying on retail-based agent networks for financial services
Users requiring both online convenience and in-person transaction capability
diagnosis: Western Union is best for unbanked and underbanked consumers who need physical locations to send money, receive cash, or pay bills, particularly immigrant communities utilizing their extensive retail agent network and multilingual support. The primary caveat is that money order availability is location-specific and inconsistent, and the company charges fees on transactions without transparent pricing displayed on their website.
services: Cash pickup services at agent locations
